description: A tensioned strand made to vibrate and produce musical sound is 'lina'. Its material may be animal gut, plant fibre, metal, or a synthetic substance. 'sima' (thread) and 'nira' (cord) name strands by other work; tension and sound make the instrument string.
articulatory_notes: Lateral 'l' releases into stressed high-front 'i'. Dental nasal 'n' then closes behind the upper teeth and opens into final 'a'.
usage_notes: An instrument or position can come first when a particular string must be identified. Gauge and material identify the string itself; tuning and safe tension belong to its present setup.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: a tensioned strand whose vibration produces musical sound
semantic_domains.creation: a fitted component made and tuned for an instrument
semantic_domains.physical: a strand held under tension so that it can vibrate
